name: sidecar
description: Claude Code plugin pack with strict concept separation. Top-level dirs: hooks/ (PreToolUse·SessionStart auto-behavior), commands/ (/slash-command invoked), skills/ (Skill tool invocable). 1 plugin = 1 concept; no mixing.

plugins.name: commons
plugins.description: UserPromptSubmit + SessionStart + PreCompact + PostCompact hook — injects a cross-project do/dont layer from commons.tape (granular `@D g1..g35` form, no tool/usage); UserPromptSubmit re-injects every turn so the layer never fades. Carries LATTICE_POLICY.md (real-limits-first SSOT).

plugins.name: prefs
plugins.description: User language preferences — 3 axes (code authoring · doc authoring · response language to user). UserPromptSubmit + SessionStart + PreCompact + PostCompact hook auto-injects current values; UserPromptSubmit re-injects every turn so the preference never fades. /prefs show · /prefs code <lang> · /prefs docs <lang> · /prefs response <lang>. Storage at $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA/prefs.json. Defaults: code=english, docs=english, response=korean. SSOT for language preferences.

plugins.name: inbox
plugins.description: Cross-project handoff inbox — skill (natural language) + /inbox list / new <kind> <slug> for filing inbox/<kind>/<slug>.md entries in the current repo.

plugins.name: hexa-lsp
plugins.description: Wire the hexa-lang LSP server (`hexa lsp`) for `.hexa` files. LSP config in plugin-root `.lsp.json` (canonical Claude Code filename, dot-prefixed). Minimal: only `.hexa` claimed.

plugins.name: tape-lsp
plugins.description: Wire `tape-lsp` (canonical .tape v1.2 LSP — diagnostics + hover) for `.tape` files. LSP config in plugin-root `.lsp.json` (canonical Claude Code filename, dot-prefixed). Requires `tape-lsp` on PATH (install via `hx install dancinlab/tape`).

plugins.name: project-tape
plugins.description: PreCompact + PostCompact hook — re-injects <project-root>/project.tape as additionalContext so the project's identity + governance survive auto-compaction. No-op when project.tape is absent.

plugins.name: git-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) deny for force-type git push operations only, implemented in hexa-lang (`_git_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`) — `git push --force` / `-f` · `git push --force-with-lease` · `git push <remote> +<refspec>` (refspec-level force). Hook-bypass (--no-verify) NOT blocked (left to user discipline). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: sidecar-lint
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) auto-lint that fires on `git commit` in any Claude Code marketplace plugin pack (any repo with .claude-plugin/marketplace.json), implemented in hexa-lang (`_sidecar_lint.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Non-blocking findings as additionalContext: stale-history patterns in staged diff (commons @D g15) · hardcoded /Users//home/ absolute paths in staged diff (commons @D g13 · sidecar @D s3) · marketplace.json plugin entry ↔ each plugin.json version drift (commons @D g22) · hook .sh missing user-exec bit. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: hexa-native
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Write|Edit|NotebookEdit) hard block for `.py` / `.sh` writes inside any project rooted at a directory containing a `project.tape` marker (sidecar's canonical project identity file), implemented in hexa-lang (`_hexa_native.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Walks up parent dirs of the target file; if a `project.tape` is found, the write is denied with a message redirecting to `.hexa` (since `.py` / `.sh` are already supported as ai-native English elsewhere). NO opt-out: no env var, no config file, no exception list, no self-exclusion — hard-coded by design. Targets only `.py` / `.sh`; other extensions pass through. Projects without `project.tape` are unaffected.

plugins.name: tape-lint
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Edit|Write) deny for `.tape` edits, implemented in hexa-lang (`_tape_lint.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run` — no Python, no shell shim). Three checks, all diff-aware (pre-existing violations grandfathered, only newly-introduced or worsened items block). (1) fields rule — `@D` blocks accept only `do` / `dont`; new `why` · `tool` · `note` · `ref` · `ex` · ... refused. Fires on any `*.tape` path. (2) length cap — `do` / `dont` value > 100 chars refused (split overflow). commons.tape + project.tape only. (3) authoring-language — when `sidecar prefs` `code` axis is `english`, lines newly introducing non-Latin scripts (Hangul · CJK · Hiragana · Katakana) are refused. commons.tape + project.tape only. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: pool-route
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) pool auto-router, implemented in hexa-lang (`_pool_route.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). When the `pool` CLI's roster (`~/.pool/pool.json`) has ≥1 enabled host and a Bash command matches the heavy classifier (make · cargo · npm · gradle · pytest · go build · swift build · docker build · nvidia-smi · train · …) or a root-needing command, it is rewritten via the hook `updatedInput` field to run on a pool host over ssh (`ssh <host> 'cd <workdir> && <cmd>'`) — transparent dispatch, not a suggestion. OS-capability filter + round-robin load balance; a preflight ssh test -d skips hosts lacking the workdir (project kept on pool hosts by the user, not synced by pool-route). No roster / no match / no eligible host → passes through untouched. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: limit-guard
plugins.description: PostToolUse(Task) hook, implemented in hexa-lang (`_limit_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`) — detects a session/usage-limit signal in a subagent result and injects a non-blocking checkpoint directive: report progress (committed SHAs vs uncommitted), commit + push uncommitted work, write a `.claude/RESUME.md` resume manifest, stop parallel fan-out and switch to sequential commit-per-unit work. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: all-bg-go
plugins.description: Parallel fan-out trigger — 'all bg go' → plan table + one background Agent per branch in the same message. REACTIVE single fan-out of what the PRIOR turn offered. For a self-generating repeatable loop, use /cycle instead. Skill (natural language) + /all-bg-go command.

plugins.name: cycle
plugins.description: Autonomous work-loop driver — /cycle runs next-list (self-enumerate next viable work from current context) → parallel-plan table → fan-out (one background Agent per item, same message) → loop. Repeat /cycle to march through a goal in parallel batches. Distinct from all-bg-go (reactive fan-out of PRIOR-turn branches; /cycle self-generates the next batch each round). NL triggers: /cycle · 사이클 · 계속 진행 · 다음 라운드 · keep cycling · march on.

plugins.name: pool
plugins.description: /pool <args> — wraps the `pool` CLI (host roster + remote exec — minimal Python, zero deps). Verbs: list · add <host> · on <host> <cmd> · status · install tailscale · rm <host>. NL trigger via skill. Pairs with commons g9 + sidecar's pool-route hook.

plugins.name: cloud
plugins.description: /cloud <args> — wraps `hexa cloud` (runpod dispatch · canonical subcommand form, structured argv — never raw ssh/scp per commons g8). Subverbs: preflight · run · nohup · poll · copy-to · copy-from. preflight = closed-form GPU mem-budget check (refuses out-of-budget specs before pod spinup · no LLM · $0). NL trigger via skill. Pairs with commons g8 + g12.

plugins.name: hexa-help
plugins.description: /hexa-help [verb] — wraps `hexa --help` (no arg, top-level catalog) or `hexa <verb> --help` (verb-specific). Per commons g7. NL trigger via skill.

plugins.name: secret
plugins.description: /secret <args> — wraps the `secret` CLI (macOS Keychain-backed credentials, dancinlab/secret 0.4.0, dual-channel sync). Verbs: get · set · rotate · check · delete · list · service · init [icloud|github <url>] · backup [enable <url>|disable|status] · sync · migrate. Two independent sync channels (iCloud Drive primary + optional private GitHub mirror) push the same encrypted blob — master password is the only decryption secret. Auto-push ON by default once `backup enable` runs (opt out via SECRET_BACKUP_AUTO=0). High-value protection on set (BIP39 wordlist-validated · xprv/WIF/64-hex auto-detect → refuse without --allow-mnemonic + stdin/tty). rotate emits sentinel only.

plugins.name: kick
plugins.description: /kick <natural-language seed> — runs `hexa kick --seed "<seed>"` (hexa-lang gap-breakthrough / discovery engine, aliased to `hexa drill`). All args join into the seed. NL trigger via skill (`kick this`, `돌파해줘`, `discover for`). Pairs with commons g6.

plugins.name: verify
plugins.description: /verify <args> — runs `hexa verify "$@"` (cross-project tier rubric, TECS-L-aligned). Forms: <id> (atlas atom), --expr <fn> <n> <v> (numerical), --fence "<claim>" (honest ⚪), rubric / list. Returns 🔵🟢🟡🟠🔴⚪ tier badge VERBATIM. NL trigger via skill (`verify this`, `확인해`, `검증해`, `맞아?`). Pairs with commons g5.

plugins.name: domain
plugins.description: Maintain UPPERCASE <NAME>.md (current snapshot) + sister <NAME>.log.md (append-only checkbox-task log) at project root — auto-scaffolds both, defaults NAME to uppercase basename of git root. Verbs: /domain (show) · /domain <task> (append `- [x]`) · /domain todo <task> (`- [ ]`) · /domain done <match> (flip [ ]→[x]) · /domain new <header>. Records progress as work proceeds — log accumulates a checkbox audit trail.

plugins.name: gap
plugins.description: /gap — multi-axis gap exploration. 42 breakthrough-strategy lenses · 8 families (F4 + F6 each have 6 lenses; `occams-razor` lives in both — hypothesis side and design side). Bare /gap = inline-triage + deep-dive only hot families. /gap full = exhaustive 8-subagent fan-out. /gap <scope> targets the sweep. /gap list prints the catalogue. Surfaces + prioritises gaps; never fixes.

plugins.name: inject
plugins.description: Immediately inject the latest sidecar commons.tape + project.tape into the CURRENT session and sync the local install for the next session. /inject runs `sidecar sync` (marketplace pull + cache copy + installed_plugins.json patch) AND prints the current commons.tape + (cwd's) project.tape so the model picks them up this turn.

plugins.name: ship
plugins.description: Atomic ship tail for sidecar plugin changes — /ship -m "<msg>" <path>... stages explicit paths (never -A/-u), credential-scans the staged diff (rpa_ · sk- · hf_ · AKIA), commits, pushes origin/<branch>, then runs `sidecar sync` (marketplace pull + cache copy + installed_plugins.json patch). Mechanical tail of @D ship / commons g27; the agent owns version bump + surface lockstep + message FIRST. NL trigger via skill (ship · 배포 · 출시).

plugins.name: bypass
plugins.description: Anti-punt — DEFAULT BEHAVIOR (auto-fires, not opt-in). Universal self-check before any move that hands control back to the user (interactive input · unauthorized destructive · external visible · explicit user-review request); if all NO, just execute. Extensible catalog of patterns: 'next user action:' blocks · 'Should I proceed?' confirmations · 'Want me to check?' deferrals · option-trees with obvious default · over-clarification · defer-by-waiting · excessive recap. Cross-project always-on enforcement in commons.tape ≥0.7.3.

plugins.name: brainstorm
plugins.description: Iterative brainstorming — given a seed, generates ideas in rounds and keeps going until depletion (no genuinely new ideas vs prior rounds; hard cap 8 rounds). For breadth over selection. Skill (natural language) + /brainstorm command.

plugins.name: easy
plugins.description: Easy (friendly) response style — 7-element pattern (icon · name · alias · plain-line · analogy · ASCII · compare). Skill (natural-language trigger) + /easy command. 5 language samples: en · ko · ja · zh · ru.

plugins.name: research
plugins.description: Research-fetch tools — `/research:arxiv <query|id>` searches the official arXiv API or fetches by id (title · authors · abstract · pdf); `/research:yt <url-or-id>` extracts YouTube caption transcript via the InnerTube ANDROID client. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_arxiv.hexa` / `_yt.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`); HTTP via curl, no API key.

plugins.name: gh-stack
plugins.description: Stacked-PR workflow skill — proposes `gh stack` commands (enabled repos) or the manual `gh pr create --base previous-layer` fallback. Encodes the <200-lines-per-layer · 1-concern governance.

plugins.name: paper
plugins.description: /paper <args> — arxiv-style LaTeX paper scaffolder. Verbs: new <slug> (scaffold template/ skeleton at ./<slug>/, ships with a working matplotlib figure example + `make figures` target) · sample <slug> (copy the bundled demiurge sample-nb-bcs-absorbed verbatim, an arxiv-quality reference exhibit) · fig <size> <prompt> <out.png> (delegates to sister `imagine` plugin → fal.ai `openai/gpt-image-2`) · compile [dir] (pdflatex × 3 + bibtex) · list (bundled samples) · help. Template is a minimal arxiv-style single-column 11pt skeleton (main.tex + references.bib + Makefile + README.md + figures/_scripts/fig01_example.py + figures/fig01_example.pdf + figures/_prompts/) — compiles end-to-end out of the box, one figure inlined. `fig` requires the sister `imagine` plugin (also in sidecar marketplace).

plugins.name: imagine
plugins.description: /imagine <prompt-file> <out.png> [-s size] [-b backend] [-m model] — generic AI image generator. Two backends out of the box: `fal` (queue+poll fal.ai, default — `secret get fal.api_key`, model = openai/gpt-image-2 firm-pinned default) and `openai` (sync /v1/images/generations — `secret get openai.api_key`, model = gpt-image-1). Canonical fal-style size tokens (`square_hd` / `landscape_16_9` / `portrait_16_9` / `square`) translate per-backend. Prompt always read from a file (provenance), payload JSON via mktemp (prompt never on argv). Backend pluggable — drop a new `_backends/<name>.hexa` to add one.
